errors: The ELM implementation has an age threshold mismatch. The CPG specifies an age range of 30-85 years, but the ELM implementation uses an age range of 30-80 years. The LDL Cholesterol threshold of 190 mg/dL matches the CPG requirement.

The HbA1c Laboratory Test value in the ELM implementation is specified as >= 9 g%, whereas the CPG requirements state that the result value should be >= 6.5%. This discrepancy indicates a mismatch between the ELM implementation and the CPG requirements.

The ELM implementation of the MeetsInclusionCriteria logic is incorrect. The CPG requires that all of the following must be true: Screening Age (age >= 40 years), Gender (female), and Mammogram (no procedure in past 1 year). However, the ELM implementation uses an OR operator instead of an AND operator. The correct implementation should be: [MeetsInclusionCriteria] = [Screening Age] AND [Gender] AND [Mammogram]. Additionally, the Mammogram logic in ELM is IsNull(ProcedureLookBack(...)), which checks if there is no mammogram procedure in the lookback period, but the CPG requires that there is no mammogram procedure in the past 1 year, which can be implemented as NOT ProcedureLookBack(...).

The ELM implementation has a logical error in the "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" definition. According to the CPG, screening is needed if the patient is 65 or older AND no falls risk assessment has been performed in the past 1 year. However, the ELM implementation defines "Needs Falls Prevention Screening" as "[In Demographic] AND [Has Recent Falls Assessment]", which means screening is needed if the patient is 65 or older AND a falls risk assessment has been performed in the past 1 year. This is the opposite of the CPG requirement. The correct definition should be "[In Demographic] AND NOT [Has Recent Falls Assessment]".

The ELM implementation of the MeetsExclusionCriteria logic is incorrect.
